 {"id": "bd-3u0", "title": "Hard-parts workload matrix: map real binaries to required subsystems", "description": "Critique mapping: #1 + #4.

Deliverables:
- Curate representative binary matrix (coreutils/python/git/curl/etc) with required subsystem dependencies.
- Use matrix to prioritize rtld/nss/locale/resolver milestones by real-world unblock impact.

Acceptance:
- Every hard-part milestone references at least one blocked workload from matrix.
- Matrix used by bv/priority report for automatic next-task ranking.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.", "notes": "2026-02-11 closure (CrimsonCove): workload_matrix_test (6 pass) + gate script. Workloads have required fields, subsystem impact consistent, milestones reference valid workloads.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "CrimsonCove", "created_at": "2026-02-11T02:48:11.032905409Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-11T16:54:16.707976Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-11T16:54:16.707976Z", "close_reason": "Hard-parts workload matrix operational. 6 harness tests pass. check_workload_matrix.sh validates workload definitions, subsystem impact, and milestones.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["critique", "e2e", "roadmap"]}

 {"id": "bd-3v3", "title": "Harness: asupersync deterministic orchestration for conformance + runtime_math scenarios", "description": "Integrate /dp/asupersync into glibc-rs-harness runner.

Scope:
- Deterministic scheduling, traceability ids, structured results.
- Orchestrate scenario runs that exercise runtime_math controllers.

Acceptance criteria:
- Harness runner uses asupersync primitives for reproducible runs and evidence logging.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "CobaltForge", "created_at": "2026-02-09T21:35:42.149982753Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-10T05:21:29.149784585Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-10T05:21:29.149765789Z", "close_reason": "done", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "comments": [{"id": 1022, "issue_id": "bd-3v3", "author": "CobaltForge", "text": "Implemented asupersync-backed deterministic conformance orchestration in glibc-rs-harness.

- Added `glibc_rs_harness::asupersync_orchestrator` (feature `asupersync-tooling`) to run fixture verification deterministically (stable fixture-set + case ordering), assign stable `trace_id`s, and emit structured evidence via `asupersync_conformance::{SuiteResult, TestResult, Checkpoint, ConformanceTestLogger}`.
- Fixed harness runner bug where fixture cases with `mode=both` were executed with mode=both (unsupported) instead of the active runner mode.
- `harness verify` now sorts fixture JSON paths deterministically and uses the orchestrator when `asupersync-tooling` is enabled; when `--report` is provided it additionally writes `<report>.suite.json` via `asupersync_conformance::write_json_report`.

Quality gates: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo check --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo test --all-targets` all pass.

+{"id":"bd-3vne","title":"syscall: process_vm_readv/writev all-NULL zero-count requests should succeed and preserve errno","description":"Host glibc returns success and leaves errno unchanged when process_vm_readv/process_vm_writev are called with both iov pointers NULL and both counts zero. Add focused ABI regression coverage so the full no-op fast path is frozen explicitly rather than only via one-sided zero-count cases.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberSignal","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:20:23.931045439Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:20:58.274467700Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "bd-3vtx", "title": "bd-1rf subtask: hosts files lookup subset implementation + explicit scope boundaries", "description": "Background:
- hosts lookups are a practical dependency for many workloads and must be deterministic in files-backend scope.

Goal:
- Implement hosts files lookup subset with explicit scope boundaries and error semantics.

Deliverables:
1) Deterministic hosts parsing and lookup.
2) API mapping for supported resolver-facing calls in files scope.
3) Explicit non-goals for networked NSS backends.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Covered host lookup fixtures pass.
- Out-of-scope behavior is explicit and documented.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for IPv4/IPv6 and malformed hosts entries.
- Structured logs: trace_id, query, family, result_source, outcome, timing.", "acceptance_criteria": "## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.", "notes": "### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "CloudyRobin", "created_at": "2026-02-12T15:01:53.995225917Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-15T19:03:24.895427810Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-15T19:02:54.796760654Z", "close_reason": "Implemented and verified (hosts files subset)", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["critique", "implementation", "nss", "resolver", "testing"], "comments": [{"id": 1023, "issue_id": "bd-3vtx", "author": "CloudyRobin", "text": "Implementation + verification evidence for `bd-3vtx`:

Implemented scope (files backend only; explicit non-goal for DNS/networked NSS fallback):
- `crates/frankenlibc-core/src/resolv/mod.rs`
  - added `getaddrinfo_with_hosts(...)`
  - retained numeric resolution path
  - added deterministic hosts-content lookup path and family filtering
  - explicit no-network fallback behavior (`EAI_NONAME` when unresolved in files subset)
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/resolv_abi.rs`
  - wired `/etc/hosts` subset resolution in ABI `getaddrinfo` for non-numeric names
- `crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs`
  - conformance executor supports optional `hosts_content` input for `getaddrinfo`
- `tests/conformance/fixtures/resolver.json`
  - added `getaddrinfo_hosts_file_subset` fixture

Commits carrying this work:
- `2a6d8aa` feat(resolv): add /etc/hosts file-backend lookup for getaddrinfo
- `ebb96f5` test(conformance): add hosts-file getaddrinfo resolver fixture

Verification (offloaded using `rch`, remote worker `vmi1152480`):
1. `RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-core getaddrinfo_with_hosts -- --nocapture`
   - Result: PASS (4 tests)
   - `getaddrinfo_with_hosts_ipv4_lookup`
   - `getaddrinfo_with_hosts_respects_family_filter`
   - `getaddrinfo_with_hosts_case_insensitive_alias`
   - `getaddrinfo_with_hosts_no_network_fallback`
2. `RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc_conformance execute_getaddrinfo_case_uses_hosts_subset_when_provided -- --nocapture`
   - Result: PASS (1 test)

Operational note: an initial `rch` run in the active shared workspace hit rsync churn due concurrent file activity by other agents and attempted local fallback; I stopped those runs and re-ran in an isolated temporary worktree to keep execution fully remote/offloaded.
", "created_at": "2026-02-15T19:02:16Z"}]}

 {"id": "bd-66wz.3", "title": "bd-66wz subtask: Pointer validation adversarial pipeline tests (free/UAF/canary/foreign)", "description": "Background
- The `ValidationPipeline` is the TSM enforcement surface: its outcomes drive Allow/Repair/Deny decisions in libc entrypoints.
- We need direct adversarial regression tests proving the pipeline never returns permissive abstractions for non-live pointers, even when caches and heuristics are involved.

Scope
- Expand unit tests in `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/ptr_validator.rs` (and, where appropriate, `arena.rs` / `fingerprint.rs`) to cover adversarial sequences and edge outcomes.
- Prefer testing via `ValidationPipeline::{allocate, free, validate}` instead of reaching into `arena` directly, so the page-oracle/bloom/TLS-cache integration is exercised.

Required Adversarial Cases
1) Foreign pointer validate: returns `Foreign(_)`, and must never claim bounded Valid/Writable remaining-bytes.
2) Foreign free: `ValidationPipeline::free()` must return `FreeResult::ForeignPointer` (and must not crash).
3) Double free: first free => `Freed` (or `FreedWithCanaryCorruption` if provoked); second free => `DoubleFree`.
4) UAF via generation: after free, `validate(addr)` must not yield `CachedValid(_)` and must yield a restrictive outcome (`TemporalViolation` or non-readable).
5) Canary corruption path: corrupt trailing canary; free returns `FreedWithCanaryCorruption`; subsequent validate for addr must remain non-readable/non-writable.
6) Cache interaction: populate TLS cache with a valid pointer; free it; validate again must miss cache and must not return a live abstraction.

Safety/Lattice Assertions
- For each case above, assert `can_read/can_write` matches expectation and that the extracted `PointerAbstraction` state is not `Valid/Writable` for freed/quarantined pointers.

Acceptance
- New tests added to `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/ptr_validator.rs` are deterministic, fast, and produce helpful failure context (case_id, sequence step).
- No new unsafe code outside the existing test intentional-overflow blocks.

Notes
- This bead is test-first: if a test fails, fix the pipeline/arena behavior with the smallest correct change and add a regression assertion.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "SilverLake", "created_at": "2026-02-13T00:57:51.973303147Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-13T01:05:41.057874399Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-13T01:05:41.057855244Z", "close_reason": "Added ptr validation adversarial tests (foreign/free/doublefree/canary) + stabilized cache-hit assertions", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["allocator", "membrane", "testing", "unit", "verification"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-66wz.3", "depends_on_id": "bd-66wz", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:32Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1053, "issue_id": "bd-66wz.3", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "Implemented adversarial `ValidationPipeline` tests for free/UAF/canary/foreign and stabilized cache-hit assertions under parallel tests.

Changes
- `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/ptr_validator.rs`:
  - Strengthened foreign-pointer test to assert `ValidationOutcome::Foreign` and `PointerAbstraction` remains `Unknown` with no bounds/generation.
  - Switched allocation/free tests to exercise `ValidationPipeline::{allocate, free}` to cover page-oracle/bloom integration.
  - Added tests: `foreign_free_reported`, `double_free_reported`, `canary_corruption_detected_via_pipeline_free` (with local `#[allow(unsafe_code)]` + `// SAFETY:` comment).
  - Wrapped cache-hit expectations with `lock_tls_cache_epoch_for_tests()` and ensured the lock is dropped before free to avoid deadlock.

Verification
- `TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane -- --nocapture` (passes).", "created_at": "2026-02-13T01:05:40Z"}]}
 {"id": "bd-66wz.4", "title": "bd-66wz subtask: Deterministic property/invariant tests for allocator+membrane state transitions", "description": "Background
- Unit tests cover specific sequences; we also need broad invariant pressure over many sequences to catch edge transitions (especially around cache invalidation and quarantine accounting).
- We require determinism (replayable seeds) and short runtime so these can run in CI on every change.

Scope
- Add deterministic property-style tests (no external RNG dependency required) for allocator+membrane state transitions.
- Prefer a tiny local PRNG (e.g. xorshift64*) with fixed seeds so failures are reproducible without environment variables.

Invariant Set (examples; encode as named invariants in assertions)
1) No stale-permissive cache: after any operation that frees an allocation, subsequent `validate(addr)` must not return `CachedValid(_)` for that addr unless a new allocation was performed and revalidated.
2) Quarantine accounting soundness: quarantine_bytes and entry counts never go negative; bounded by max constraints after drain.
3) Temporal safety while tracked: if an allocation has been freed and is still tracked in quarantine/arena, `can_read/can_write` for that addr is false.
4) Determinism: given the same seed and step count, the sequence of operations and observed outcomes is identical.

Test Plan
- Generate N sequences across a small set of fixed seeds.
- Each step randomly chooses: allocate(size), free(ptr), validate(ptr), corrupt_canary(ptr) (guarded by ptr live), etc.
- Track a simple shadow model for expected permissiveness (live vs freed-tracked vs foreign) and assert pipeline outcomes respect it.

Acceptance
- Property/invariant tests live in `crates/frankenlibc-membrane` (module choice: `arena.rs`/`ptr_validator.rs`/new `tests/` module) and run under `cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane`.
- Runtime is bounded and deterministic (<2s total locally).
- On failure, output includes: seed, step index, op, ptr addr, expected class, actual outcome.

Notes
- If adding a crate like `proptest` is considered later, keep this deterministic harness as the baseline (proptest can be an additive layer, not a replacement).", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "SilverLake", "created_at": "2026-02-13T00:58:27.206444031Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-13T01:10:36.404134097Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-13T01:10:36.404114891Z", "close_reason": "Added deterministic invariant sequence property tests", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["allocator", "membrane", "property", "testing", "verification"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-66wz.4", "depends_on_id": "bd-66wz", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:32Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1054, "issue_id": "bd-66wz.4", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "Implemented deterministic property/invariant sequence pressure tests without adding external RNG dependencies.

Changes
- Added integration test: `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/tests/allocator_membrane_invariants_sequences_test.rs`
  - Deterministic xorshift64* PRNG with fixed seeds.
  - Runs bounded sequences (alloc/free/validate/double-free) over a small slot pool and asserts core invariants:
    - live pointers validate as `CachedValid`/`Validated` and are readable+writable
    - freed-but-tracked pointers validate as `TemporalViolation` and are not readable+writable
    - second free reports `DoubleFree`
    - foreign pointers validate as `Foreign` with `SafetyState::Unknown` and no claimed bounds
  - Runtime bounded (~0.2s) and failure context includes seed/step/op.

Verification
- `TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane -- --nocapture` (unit + integration tests pass).", "created_at": "2026-02-13T01:10:36Z"}]}

 {"id": "bd-6yd", "title": "Crash bundle: deterministic evidence + backtrace capture on failure", "description": "Critique mapping: #1 (prove; do not hand-wave).

Deliverables:
- When any smoke/fixture fails, capture:
  - backtrace (RUST_BACKTRACE=1)
  - mode + env vars
  - evidence ledger snapshot (TSM repairs/denials)
  - allocator stats snapshot (arena/quarantine)
  - /proc/self/maps excerpt for pointer provenance debugging

Acceptance:
- Bundle generation is deterministic and bounded (no unbounded logs).
- Bundle is sufficient to reproduce the failure locally.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.", "notes": "2026-02-11 closure (CrimsonCove): crash_bundle_test (9 pass) + gate script.  {"id": "bd-kom", "title": "RaptorQ Runtime: Evidence symbol record format (systematic ring buffer)", "description": "Define an in-memory evidence log format inspired by FrankenSQLite SymbolRecord.

Requirements:
- Self-describing enough to decode offline: epoch_id/seed, symbol_size T, K_source, ESI, checksum.
- Systematic flag for contiguous runs.
- Auth tag optional (likely off by default; on for untrusted export paths).

Deliverables:
- A concrete struct layout (packed/align-safe) and invariants.
- Mapping from runtime events -> byte payload.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "CobaltForge", "created_at": "2026-02-09T21:34:56.179506346Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-10T15:59:32.230342536Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-10T15:59:32.230323721Z", "close_reason": "done (record format + ring buffer + payload mapping implemented; see evidence.rs + format doc)", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-kom", "depends_on_id": "bd-3a9", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:33Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1104, "issue_id": "bd-kom", "author": "GrayPond", "text": "Implemented bd-kom EvidenceSymbolRecord v1 + systematic ring buffer and payload mapping.

Delivered:
- Stable 256-byte record format (explicit offsets, LE), no repr(packed)/no unsafe: crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/evidence.rs
- Byte-level format spec + payload schema (EVR1/EVP1): crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/evidence_symbol_record_format.md
- Overwrite-on-full publication semantics + snapshot protocol; per-(mode,family) epoch stream state + hash chaining: crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/evidence.rs
- Module wired into runtime_math namespace: crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs

Notes:
- Repair-symbol generation is explicitly deferred to bd-1es (cadence-only) and integration to bd-3ku.
- All work is merged in commit 864395b.", "created_at": "2026-02-10T15:59:29Z"}]}

Add focused ABI regression tests for that validation order on both readv and writev.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:20:33.711515157Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:20:33.711515157Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "bd-kx74", "title": "EPIC: Membrane activation and runtime policy hardening", "description": "The Transparent Safety Membrane (TSM) validation pipeline is currently in permanent passthrough mode under LD_PRELOAD. signal_runtime_ready() was removed from startup because activating the membrane caused hangs and crashes due to re-entrancy in the validation pipeline.

CURRENT STATE:
- runtime_policy::decide() always returns passthrough (RUNTIME_READY is never set to 1)
- All ABI functions run without membrane validation
- The membrane code exists and works in tests, but is disabled at runtime

ROOT CAUSE: The ValidationPipeline uses RwLock internally. When an interposed function (e.g., malloc) goes through the pipeline, and the pipeline calls a function that itself is interposed (causing re-entry), the RwLock deadlocks.

SOLUTION APPROACH:
1. Make the pipeline re-entrant (use try_read instead of read, fast-path on contention)
2. OR: Only activate membrane after all bootstrap is complete and all interposed functions have host delegation fallbacks
3. OR: Use a per-thread re-entrancy flag to skip validation when already inside the pipeline

This is the next major milestone after the current LD_PRELOAD compatibility work.

RATIONALE: The TSM is the core innovation of FrankenLibC. Without it active, we're just a passthrough libc wrapper. Activating it is essential for the project's value proposition.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "feature", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:06:54.923380535Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-02T19:48:49.201570576Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-02T19:48:49.201289334Z", "close_reason": "All children closed: bd-kx74.1 (per-thread reentracy guard) and bd-kx74.2 (signal_runtime_ready re-enabled). Membrane activation and runtime policy hardening complete.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "bd-kx74.1", "title": "Implement per-thread re-entrancy guard for membrane validation pipeline", "description": "Add a thread-local boolean flag MEMBRANE_REENTRY that skips validation when a thread is already inside the pipeline. This prevents the RwLock deadlock that caused signal_runtime_ready to be removed.

APPROACH:
1. Add thread_local! { static MEMBRANE_ACTIVE: Cell<bool> = Cell::new(false); }
2. In runtime_policy::decide(), check MEMBRANE_ACTIVE first — if true, return passthrough
3. Set MEMBRANE_ACTIVE to true before entering the pipeline, false after
4. This allows nested calls (malloc inside validation) to bypass the pipeline

RISK: Some functions SHOULD be validated even during nested calls. The per-thread flag is a coarse solution. A more nuanced approach would be per-API-family re-entrancy tracking.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:06:55.120006400Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-25T03:46:37.715103006Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-25T03:46:37.714070612Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-kx74.1", "depends_on_id": "bd-kx74", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:06:55.120006400Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
 {"id": "bd-kx74.2", "title": "Re-enable signal_runtime_ready() in startup paths", "description": "Once the re-entrancy guard is in place, re-enable signal_runtime_ready() in both:
1. host_delegate_main_wrapper (startup_abi.rs:422)
2. The fallback __libc_start_main path (startup_abi.rs:855)

TEST: Run the full 61-program suite + complex programs with membrane active. Verify no hangs or crashes. Measure overhead.

DEPENDS ON: Re-entrancy guard must be implemented first.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:06:56.339062891Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-25T03:46:58.606068078Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-25T03:46:58.605102992Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-kx74.2", "depends_on_id": "bd-kx74", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:06:56.339062891Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-kx74.2", "depends_on_id": "bd-kx74.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:06:57.559397522Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-kx74.2", "depends_on_id": "bd-s39o", "type": "related", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:08:26.058213823Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}

 {"id": "bd-reax.1", "title": "Add host delegation for critical RPC functions (xdr_*, clnt_*)", "description": "FILES: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/rpc_abi.rs
CURRENT: ~68 RPC symbols use rpc_native! macro returning safe defaults (0/NULL).
PROBLEM: Programs using Sun RPC (portmap, NFS tools, ypbind) get silent failures.
FIX: For the most commonly used RPC functions (clntudp_create, clnttcp_create, xdr_int, xdr_string, etc.), resolve the host glibc symbol via our ELF resolver and delegate. Keep the safe-default fallback for symbols not found in host.
CONSIDERATION: RPC is deprecated and being replaced by gRPC. Many modern systems don't even have RPC tools installed. This is low priority unless a specific program needs it.", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "feature", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:04:56.142778940Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-25T19:17:39.021445416Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-25T19:17:39.020480090Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-reax.1", "depends_on_id": "bd-reax", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:04:56.142778940Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
 {"id": "bd-reax.2", "title": "Add IFUNC relocation support to ELF loader", "description": "FILES: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/elf/loader.rs:222
CURRENT: Only Phase-1 relocations supported. IFUNC (STT_GNU_IFUNC) relocations are deferred.
PROBLEM: glibc itself uses IFUNC for CPU-specific optimized versions of memcpy, memset, etc. When we try to resolve these from the host ELF, we may get the IFUNC resolver instead of the final address.
FIX: Detect IFUNC symbols and call the resolver function to get the actual implementation address.
IMPACT: Affects host_resolve.rs which parses the glibc ELF to find symbols like malloc, dlvsym, etc. If any of these are IFUNCs, we'd get the wrong address.
CONSIDERATION: On modern x86_64 glibc, malloc and dlvsym are NOT IFUNCs (they're direct symbols), but memcpy/memset ARE IFUNCs. Since we only resolve non-IFUNC symbols from host_resolve.rs, this may not be an immediate problem.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "feature", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:04:57.285183747Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-25T19:15:50.515692509Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-25T19:15:50.514670096Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-reax.2", "depends_on_id": "bd-reax", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:04:57.285183747Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}

 {"id": "bd-rth1", "title": "bd-yos subtask: TLS handoff and teardown correctness for spawned threads", "description": "Background:
- TLS handoff correctness is required for thread-local errno and future pthread_key destructor behavior.

Goal:
- Implement and verify TLS initialization/teardown handoff for spawned threads.

Deliverables:
1) TLS init protocol during thread start.
2) Per-thread errno/TLS storage linkage checks.
3) Teardown sequencing and cleanup invariants.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Thread-local values remain isolated and stable under concurrent workloads.
- TLS teardown leaves no leaked state.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for TLS setup/teardown and isolation.
- E2E concurrent tests validating cross-thread isolation.
- Structured logs with trace_id, thread_id, tls_event, key_count, cleanup_status.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "Codex", "created_at": "2026-02-12T15:00:52.128037997Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-22T06:12:33.353261867Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-22T06:12:33.353242521Z", "close_reason": "Completed TLS handoff/teardown correctness fixes and verification evidence posted in comments.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["critique", "implementation", "pthread", "testing", "tls"], "comments": [{"id": 1142, "issue_id": "bd-rth1", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "Codex claiming this now. Found a concrete TLS table correctness bug: removing one TID currently breaks linear-probe lookup chains for colliding TIDs, which can corrupt per-thread TLS lookup after teardown. I am implementing tombstone-based delete + collision tests now.", "created_at": "2026-02-22T05:19:32Z"}, {"id": 1143, "issue_id": "bd-rth1", "author": "Codex", "text": "Completed TLS handoff/teardown hardening and verification.

- Added ABI integration tests in :

 {"id": "bd-s39o", "title": "EPIC: Expand LD_PRELOAD program compatibility beyond 61/61 core suite", "description": "FrankenLibC currently passes 61/61 programs in the core LD_PRELOAD test suite PLUS 12+ complex programs (Python, Perl, Node.js, SSH, OpenSSL, Bash, Zsh, Vim, less, nano, curl, make, tar, gzip).

GOAL: Systematically expand compatibility to cover more real-world workloads. The next targets should be programs that exercise features we haven't fully tested:
- Database clients (psql, mysql, sqlite3)
- Build tools (cmake, ninja, gcc, rustc)
- Network tools (wget, dig, nslookup)
- Compression (bzip2, xz, zstd)
- Package managers (apt, dpkg)
- Editors/pagers (emacs, man)

APPROACH: Test each program, capture failure backtraces, fix root causes. Each fix typically benefits many programs.

ALREADY WORKING (verified):
61 core: cat ls echo wc head tail sort grep sed awk date env id uname whoami hostname pwd basename dirname mkdir rmdir touch rm cp mv ln chmod stat file dd tr cut paste fold uniq comm tee xargs yes seq expr test true false printf readlink realpath nproc getconf stty tty logname uptime arch git df du free kill sleep nice
Complex: python3 perl node curl make tar gzip ssh openssl bash zsh vim less nano", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "feature", "assignee": "PlumCove", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:05:51.740882739Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-02T19:47:50.422019583Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-02T19:47:50.421726608Z", "close_reason": "All children closed: bd-s39o.1 (database clients), bd-s39o.2 (build tools), bd-s39o.3 (network tools). 61/61 core suite + 12+ complex programs pass.
